Erica Scarpa
CV

CV

Erica Scarpa – Curriculum vitae

Personal information

  • e-mail: erica.scarpa@gmail.com; erica.scarpa@unive.it
  • Web pages: GitHub; academia.edu; ResearchGate

Education

  • 2020: PhD (Dottorato di ricerca) in Ancient Heritage Studies, Ca’ Foscari University of Venice (XXXII cycle). Supervisor: Prof. Lucio Milano. Dissertation: Social History through Textual Patterns. Study on the Social Organization at Ebla during the Age of the Archives (Syria, 24th century BCE) (Link)
  • 2013: Master’s Degree (Laurea Magistrale) in Ancient Heritage Studies, Ca’ Foscari University of Venice (Italy). Supervisor: Prof. Lucio Milano. Thesis: La geografia storica della Siria nell’età degli Archivi di Ebla, 110/110 cum laude (Link)
  • 2010: Bachelor’s Degree (Laurea Triennale) in Archaeology, Università degli Studi di Padova (Italy). Supervisor: Prof. Giovanni Battista Lanfranchi. Thesis: La Maledizione di Akkad. Tra ricostruzione storica ed interpretazione religiosa. 108/110

Publications

  • Scarpa, Erica (2017). The City of Ebla. A Complete Bibliography of its Archaeological and Textual Remains. Venezia: Edizioni Ca’ Foscari. (Open Access; GitHub Repository; Online version).
  • Scarpa, Erica (2016). «Untangling the Past: l’assiriologia e le reti sociali come strumento di indagine storica». In: Rui, M. (a cura di), Design the Future! Extended abstracts della multiconferenza EMEMITALIA2016, Modena, 7-9 settembre 2016. Genova University Press. Genova, pp. 413-422. (Link).

Conference Papers

  • «Don’t Fear, Don’t Trust: Some Methodological Considerations on Data Mining and Data Visualization Applied to Cuneiform Sources». Thinking Digital in Cuneiform Studies: Methods, Problems, Perspectives (March 27th-28th, Ca’ Foscari University of Venice) (Link).
  • «The maškim Network». LXIII Rencontre Assyriologique Internationale, Dealing with Antiquity – Past, Present, and Future (July 24th-28th 2017, Philipps-Universität, Marburg) (Link).

Teaching

  • Sept – Dec 2018: Tutoring – Supplementary teaching activity in Assyriology, Ca’ Foscari University of Venice. Course: Assiriology I and II (a.y. 2018/2019), Prof. Paola Corò.
  • Nov – Dec 2016: Seminar – Bibliografia e Bibliografie in Orientalistica (10 hours; November 9th – December 7th 2016).
  • Feb – Mar 2017: Seminar – Information Overload. Strumenti informatici per raccogliere, estrarre e visualizzare dati nelle discipline storiche ed epigrafiche (10 hours; February 9th – March 16th 2017).
  • Sept – Dec 2015: Tutoring – Supplementary teaching activity in Assyriology, Ca’ Foscari University of Venice. Course: Assiriology I and II (a.y. 2015/2016), Prof. Paola Corò.
  • Sept – Dec 2014: Tutoring – Supplementary teaching activity in Assyriology, Ca’ Foscari University of Venice. Course: Assiriology I and II (a.y. 2014/2015), Prof. Paola Corò.
  • Mar – May 2014: Seminar – Ebla, dalla bibliografia agli archivi digitali (10 hours).
  • Nov 2013 – Mar 2014: Seminar – «Possa ogni re futuro (…) osservare le parole di giustizia che ho iscritto sulla mia stele»: Laboratorio di lettura di testi cuneiformi paleo-babilonesi​ (20 hours).

Research Contracts / Employment

  • Jun – Aug 2017: Teaching Fellow, Ca’ Foscari Harvard Summer School (Italy). Course: Venice and the Classical Past. Scientific supervision: Prof. Lorenzo Calvelli.
  • Jan – Jun 2016: Research Fellow – Indexing and prosopographical analysis of proper names beginning in D/T, Università degli Studi di Firenze (Italy). The Prosopography of Ebla Project. Scientific supervision: Prof. Amalia Catagnoti.
  • Jul - Aug 2014: Responsible for Data Econding, Ca’ Foscari University of Venice. The Ebla Digital Archives Project. Scientific supervision: Prof. Lucio Milano.
  • May - Aug 2013: Responsible for Data Econding, Ca’ Foscari University of Venice. The Ebla Digital Archives Project. Scientific supervision: Prof. Lucio Milano.
